2605.00776.title: Directed Social Regard: Surfacing Targeted Advocacy, Opposition, Aid, Harms, and Victimization in Online Media
2605.00776.paper_summary: The language in online platforms, influence operations, and political rhetoric frequently directs a mix of pro-social sentiment (e.g., advocacy, helpfulness, compassion) and anti-social sentiment (e.g., threats, opposition, blame) at different topics, all in the same message. While many natural language processing (NLP) tools classify or score a text's overall sentiment as positive, neutral, or negative, these tools cannot report that positive and negative sentiments coexist, and they cannot report the target of those sentiments. This paper presents the Directed Social Regard (DSR) approach to multi-dimensional, multi-valence sentiment analysis, comprised of a pair of transformer-based models that (1) detects span-level targets of sentiment in a message and then (2) scores all spans within the message context along three (-1, 1) axes of regard that are motivated by social science theories of moral disengagement and moral framing. We present a data collection and annotation strategy for DSR dataset construction, a transformer-based architecture for span-level scoring, and a validation study with promising results. We apply the validated DSR model on six third-party datasets of online media and report meaningful correlations between DSR outputs and the labels and topics in these pre-existing social science datasets.
2605.00776.paper_summary_zh: 在線平台、影響操作和政治修辭中的語言經常在同一信息中針對不同主題傳達一種混合的親社會情感（例如，倡導、幫助、同情）和反社會情感（例如，威脅、反對、指責）。雖然許多自然語言處理（NLP）工具將文本的整體情感分類或評分為正面、中立或負面，但這些工具無法報告正面和負面情感共存的情況，也無法報告這些情感的目標。本文提出了定向社會關注（DSR）方法，用於多維度、多價值的情感分析，該方法由一對基於Transformer的模型組成，這些模型（1）檢測信息中的情感跨度級目標，然後（2）在信息上下文中沿著三個（-1, 1）關注軸對所有跨度進行評分，這些軸是由社會科學的道德脫離和道德框架理論所驅動。我們提出了一種數據收集和標註策略，用於DSR數據集的構建，一種基於Transformer的架構，用於跨度級評分，以及一項具有良好結果的驗證研究。我們在六個第三方的在線媒體數據集上應用經驗證的DSR模型，並報告DSR輸出與這些現有社會科學數據集中的標籤和主題之間的有意義的相關性。
2605.00776.author: Scott Friedman et.al.
2605.00776.authors: Scott Friedman, Ruta Wheelock, Sonja Schmer-Galunder, Drisana Iverson, Jake Vasilakes, Joan Zheng, Jeffrey Rye, Vasanth Sarathy, Christopher Miller
